prompt: Develop a LeaseOps Insight & Assessment system for property management organizations that need offline-ready leasing analytics, controlled reporting, and on-site assessment logistics. Users sign in with a local username and password and land in a Vue.js workspace tailored by role: System Administrator configures templates, metrics, and permissions; Leasing Operations Managers oversee listings and performance; Test Proctors run on-site test sessions for agent certification and compliance; Analysts build and share reports; Standard Users (leasing agents) receive tasks and complete assigned actions. The interface provides a Notification and Task Reminder Center that aggregates approvals, overdue items, and missing materials into a single inbox with clear status chips, read/unread counts, and snooze controls; each user can opt into in-app popups only or also generate outbound “email/SMS/enterprise IM” message packages as downloadable files for manual delivery in fully offline environments, with blacklist controls to suppress recipients and a preference rule that quiet hours run from 9:00 PM to 7:00 AM local time. The Test Center & Resource Management area maintains ledgers for sites, rooms, seats, and equipment with seat maps and a utilization calendar; creating a session enforces capacity (no overbooking), a 10-minute setup buffer between sessions in the same room, and rules-based auto-allocation that prefers contiguous seating and flags accessibility seats as reserved unless explicitly released. The analytics experience combines an Analytics and Reporting Hub plus data visual analytics: users can pivot participation, attendance, hour distribution, retention, staffing gaps, event popularity, and rankings by region/community/team, then export CSV/Excel/PDF and schedule report generation daily at 6:00 AM, weekly Mondays at 7:00 AM, or monthly on the 1st at 8:00 AM, with permission-controlled sharing that prevents forwarding exports to unauthorized roles. On the system side, the backend uses Express (TypeScript) to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js frontend over a local network or single-machine deployment, with MySQL as the local system-of-record for listings, communities, regions, geo-points (stored as latitude/longitude), test resources, sessions, notifications, templates, and report definitions. Listing & Lease Metrics Modeling is implemented as versioned metric definitions with effective dates; recalculation runs on demand and nightly at 2:00 AM, producing derived measures such as unit rent, price change percentage, volatility (rolling 30-day standard deviation), vacancy/listing duration (days on market), and supply-demand ratio, with each metric version locked once used in a published report. Notification and messaging supports variable rendering with a template preview, an outbound queue with up to 3 retries at 5, 15, and 60 minutes, and delivery receipts recorded as “delivered,” “failed,” or “manually sent,” with failure alerts appearing in-app for Administrators only. Security is offline-first: passwords are salted and hashed (bcrypt), sessions expire after 30 minutes of inactivity, sensitive fields such as employee identifiers are AES-encrypted at rest, and exports watermark the viewer’s name and timestamp to discourage misuse. All actions that change allocations, metric definitions, or report sharing generate immutable audit logs retained for 7 years in MySQL.
